subject: [PATCH v10 06/15] mm: add MM_CP_UFFD_RWP change_protection() flag

2026-07-08.patch_summary: This patch adds two new flags to the change_protection() function in the Linux kernel: MM_CP_UFFD_RWP and MM_CP_UFFD_RWP_RESOLVE. The MM_CP_UFFD_RWP flag is used for userfaultfd RWP protection, which sets a PTE to PAGE_NONE with the uffd bit set, making it distinguishable from other faults. The MM_CP_UFFD_RWP_RESOLVE flag is used to restore the original vma->vm_page_prot and clear the uffd bit after RWP protection has been applied. This patch also updates several functions in the kernel to use these new flags, including change_pte_range(), change_huge_pmd(), and hugetlb_change_protection(). The goal of this patch is to provide a consistent way for userfaultfd RWP protection across different memory types, such as anonymous, shared memory, transparent huge pages, and hugetlb.
